summary: Iteration 197: Verified all 3 user-requested fixes - PipelineStalenessAlert BOLD BLUE styling, ALL export TOCs BOLD UPPERCASE format, and Quick Brief opens in browser as PDF view.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix #1 (PipelineStalenessAlert BOLD BLUE): VERIFIED - Component uses bg-blue-50, border-2 border-blue-600, text-blue-700, font-bold. Confirmed in PipelineStalenessAlert.jsx lines 44-87 and rendered in CaseDetail.jsx Progress tab line 1674.

Fix #2 (ALL Export TOCs BOLD UPPERCASE): VERIFIED - All 4 locations updated: exportHtml.js (.toc-heading 13pt/800, .toc-item 12pt/700/uppercase), BarristerView.jsx (lines 421-428), ReportView.jsx (line 924), CaseDetail.jsx (lines 915-917 and 1007-1011).
Fix #3 (Quick Brief opens in browser): VERIFIED - handleQuickBrief uses window.open(directUrl, '_blank') instead of blob download. Confirmed in BarristerView.jsx lines 735-744.
UI Test: PipelineStalenessAlert visible in Progress tab with classes 'rounded-lg border-2 border-blue-600 bg-blue-50 p-4 mb-4'
UI Test: Barrister View TOC shows 'CONTENTS (15 SECTIONS)' with numbered items in 2-column grid layout
UI Test: Quick Brief button enabled and visible in Barrister View header
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 197 verified 3 user-requested fixes: (1) PipelineStalenessAlert restored with BOLD BLUE styling (bg-blue-50, border-blue-600, text-blue-700, font-bold) - visible in Progress tab. (2) ALL 4 export TOC locations use identical BOLD UPPERCASE format: heading 13pt/800 weight, items 12pt/700 weight/uppercase in 2-column grid. (3) Quick Brief uses window.open() to display PDF in browser instead of downloading blob. Test case: R v Homann (case_ba08d8e0ad0d).
